revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Izawa-no-miya]][[ja:伊雑宮]][[zh:伊雜宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Shima, Mie, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izawa-no-miya
伊雑宮 | image = Izawa-no-miya 07.jpg | caption = Izawa-no-miya {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|22|49|N|136|48|32|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = Kaminogō, Isobe-cho, Shima-shi, Mie-ken | website = | architecture_style = ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Izawa-no-miya'''|伊雑宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Kaminogō neighborhood of {{ill|Isobe, Mie|en|Isobe, Mie|qid=Q11588089|lt=Isobe|1=Isobe, Mie}} in the city of {{ill|Shima, Mie|en|Shima, Mie|qid=Q338009|lt=Shima|1=Shima, Mie}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is one of the two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|qid=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}. Together with the {{nihongo|Takihara-no-Miya|瀧原宮|}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|en|Taiki, Mie|qid=Q1203189|lt=Taiki|1=Taiki, Mie}}, it is one of the {{nihongo|'''Amaterasu-Ōkami no Tonomiya'''|天照大神の遙宮||}}, or external branches of the Inner Shrine of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]].{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Izawa-no-miya is: * {{nihongo|'''[[Amaterasu| Amaterashimasusume-Ōkami-no-Mitama]]'''| 天照坐皇大御神御魂|}}, one of the ''{{ill|Mitama|en|Mitama|qid=Q495568|lt=Mitama|1=Mitama}}'' of the Sun Goddess ==History== The origins of the Izawa-no-miya are unknown. According to the spurious {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} {{nihongo|''Yamatohime Seki''|倭姫命世記||}}, the shrine was founded by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=PrincessYamato|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}, the daughter of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}} and first ''[[saiō]]'' of the Ise Grand Shrine, who sought a place of sacrifice further east from Ise, and this was the only land in the area with rice fields. While this legend is unsupported, the earliest mention of the shrine is in the 804 {{nihongo|Kotai Jingu Ceremony Book|皇太神宮儀式帳|Kōtaijingū Gishikichō}} and the 927 ''[[Engishiki]]'' records. The shrine was looted and burned down by forces from [[Kumano shrine]] during the {{ill|Genpei War|en|Genpei War|qid=Q737542|lt=Genpei War|1=Genpei War}} in 1180. During the [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]], the priests of this shrine forged a document attempting the "prove" that their shrine was the original Ise Grand Shrine and that the existing Ise Grand Shrine was an imposter. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was regarded as a part of Ise Grand Shrine and was not given a Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The sacred rice planting ceremony held annually on June 24 is considered to be one of Japan's three major rice planting festivals, along with [[Katori Jingū]] and [[Sumiyoshi-taisha|Sumiyoshi Taisha]]. The ceremony became a National [[:en:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 1990.{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/93|title=磯部の御神田|trans-title=Isobe no Mikamida|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}} The shrine is located a five-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminogō Station|en|Kaminogō Station|qid=Q1447297|lt=Kaminogō Station|1=Kaminogō Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Shima Line|Kintetsu Shima Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Izawa-no-miya, haraedokoro and imibiyaden.jpg|Purification spot image:Izawa-no-miya 08.jpg|Ablution font image:Izawa-no-miya 06.jpg|Torii File:Izawa-no-miya, shinden-2.jpg|Sacred rice fields image:Izawa-mitaue_03.JPG|Rice Planting festival image:Izawa-mitaue_01.JPG|Rice Planting festival ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|en|List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|qid=Q111360896|lt=List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|1=List of Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commonscat| Izawa-no-miya}} *[https://www.iseshima-kanko.jp/en/see-and-do/1294 Ise Shime Convention & Tourism official site] *[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/about/naiku/izawa.html Ise Jingu Official site]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.kankomie.or.jp/report/detail_379.htmlMie Prefecture Official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Ichinomiya}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|伊雑宮|version=101820779|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[en:Izawa-no-miya]] [[zh:%E4%BC%8A%E9%9B%9C%E5%AE%AE]] [[ja:伊雑宮]] {{Commons category|Izawa-no-miya}} [[zh:伊雜宮]] {{Commons category|Izawa-no-miya}} [[zh:伊雜宮]] {{Commons category|Izawa-no-miya}} [[zh:伊雜宮]] {{wikidata link|Q10885171}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Japan completed in 2014]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Shima, Mie]] [[Category:History of Shima, Mie]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shima Province]] [[Category:Shima Province]] [[Category:Shima, Mie]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
